# Transcrição: How_to_give_feedback_without_appearing_frustrated

- **Fonte:** How_to_give_feedback_without_appearing_frustrated
- **Duração:** 01:19
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:19]

So many people don't understand how to give feedback to others without appearing frustrated. So when you think about frustration, what is it? Frustration isn't just a selection of words, frustration is actually a melody. You'll find that when someone is frustrated the way they speak tends to be quite blunt, the words tend to end abruptly, and also on top of this the tempo seems to be a little bit sporadic, and the emotions behind it is the emotion of stress. Let me give you an example of a frustrated tone. Julie, you've sent me an email the last four days, I know you really need me to get back to you, I know it's important, but Julie my plate is completely full right now, I'll get back to you tomorrow. Kind of notice the frustration in my melody? It's basically you raising your voice without you varying your pitch. The moment you vary your pitch you sound less upset. So if I said the same thing now with a better melody it would sound like this. Julie, you've sent me an email in the last four days in a row and hey I get that what you're sending to me is really important and you need to get back to the client, but Julie my plate has been so full the last four days I'll get back to you tomorrow morning okay. So the frustration isn't just voiced via the selection of words, frustration tends to be voiced via your melody, via your lack of melody I should say, and your volume. So all you need to do is introduce more melody, introduce more pitches, and you'll sound less like you're frustrated.
